description:
## What
Deploy or stub GoToSocial locally (Docker) and run the compat-test sequence: Follow, Post, Reply, Boost, Like, DM, Media, Edit, Delete. Fix any AP shape mismatches. GoToSocial has known deviations: no `sensitive` field on Announce, strict HTTP signature requirements (signs GETs), slightly different `@context` array. Document green/gap rows in `docs/compat.md`.

## Why
GoToSocial is a major alternative implementation with growing adoption. It has stricter HTTP signature requirements than mastodon.social — specifically, it requires signed GETs for actor fetches. Getting this right validates our outbound signature code.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read GoToSocial AP implementation notes (https://docs.gotosocial.org/en/latest/federation/); capture live GoToSocial responses for actor fetch, inbox POST shapes; document all deviations from Mastodon AP shapes.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add signed GET support to all outbound actor fetch calls (required by GoToSocial); fix any AP shape mismatches in outbound activities; add GoToSocial-specific inbound shape handling where needed.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: add GoToSocial fixture files in `tests/fixtures/gotosocial/`; write replay tests asserting correct DB state; verify signed GET is used in outbound actor fetches via a test that checks the request signature header.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: GoToSocial AP implementation notes + live GoToSocial instance responses; the GoToSocial source code at https://github.com/superseriousbusiness/gotosocial is the authoritative shape reference.
- Test approach: integration test with local GoToSocial Docker instance for acceptance; fixture replay in CI.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- All outbound actor fetches must be signed (GoToSocial rejects unsigned GETs with 401).
- Must accept GoToSocial's `@context` array which includes extra extensions.
- Announce without `sensitive` field must not error — treat missing field as `false`.
- Must handle GoToSocial's `application/activity+json; charset=utf-8` Content-Type (strip charset parameter).
title: GoToSocial federation compatibility
